steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what can be inferred about Ramchand. The key word is ‘guiltily’ — Ramchand notices that all the other shop assistants are already in place. Guilt signals he feels he has fallen short of a standard.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'remorseful about arriving to work later than other shop assistants': The word ‘guiltily’ directly supports remorse, and the other assistants being ‘already in place’ confirms he is late. ✓
  • Choice B — 'mesmerized by frying jalebi batter': The jalebi detail describes the scene; nothing says Ramchand is captivated by it. ✗
  • Choice C — 'overwhelmed by chaos of other assistants selling goods': The assistants have ‘fixed, attentive smiles’ — no chaos is described. ✗
  • Choice D — 'happy after seeing satisfied shop assistants': Ramchand feels guilty, not happy. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
